mission.title: pushgateway: Allow timeout for metrics
mission.description: Allow timeout for metrics. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current pushgateway deployment
steps.description:
Verify your pushgateway version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n pushgateway -l app.kubernetes.io/name=pushgateway
helm list -n pushgateway 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working pushgateway installation.

steps.title: Review pushgateway configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant pushgateway configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n pushgateway -l app.kubernetes.io/name=pushgateway
kubectl get configmap -n pushgateway -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=pushgateway
```
In some scenarios, a client will stop pushing metrics because it has gone away. Currently every node needs to be deleted explicitly or the last value will stick around forever.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Allow timeout for metrics
steps.description:
Since the statefulset itself is fundamentally long-running and discoverable via Kubernetes SD (which gives you all the discovery metadata benefits), it seems like this is a similar case as using the Node Exporter's textfile module for metrics tied to a specific host (just that here it's a statefulset's pod and not a host). So I'd expect the recommended thing to do would be to have a sidecar in each pod that serves metrics  (either a specialized exporter or the Node Exporter with only textfile

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n pushgateway -l app.kubernetes.io/name=pushgateway
kubectl get events -n pushgateway --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Allow timeout for metrics" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Since the statefulset itself is fundamentally long-running and discoverable via Kubernetes SD (which gives you all the discovery metadata benefits), it seems like this is a similar case as using the Node Exporter's textfile module for metrics tied to a specific host (just that here it's a statefulset's pod and not a host).

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with pushgateway installed or the issue environment reproducible.
